metadata.author: douglasswm
metadata.notes: Speed camera reading disputed on radar interference grounds. Tests rebuttable presumption of accuracy + interference from emergency vehicle.

raw_documents.content: On 12 March 2026 at 19:45 I was driving on the BKE northbound at the posted limit of 80 km/h. The speed camera at km 14.2 recorded my vehicle at 121 km/h, alleging exceeding by 41 km/h. At the time of capture an SCDF ambulance overtook me on the right shoulder. My dashcam timestamp confirms the ambulance was 6 metres ahead. Radar systems are documented to lock onto larger faster targets. The reading must have been the ambulance, not my vehicle.

Camera SC-BKE-14.2 captured vehicle SBK 9018C at 121 km/h on 12 Mar 2026 19:45:08. The radar Doppler signature is consistent with the captured vehicle's apparent length and lane position. Calibration certificate dated 1 Feb 2026 attached. Section 63(2) presumes accuracy unless rebutted by primary evidence. SCDF dispatch records show no emergency call in the area between 19:30 and 20:00 on that date.

legal_rules: Road Traffic Act 1961 s.63 (exceeding speed limit)
Road Traffic Act 1961 s.63(2) (rebuttable presumption of camera accuracy)
precedents: Public Prosecutor v Tan Boon Hock
